title: Add section-based editing to knowledge edit command (--replace-section, --append-to-section)

comments.author: maxine--plan-050
comments.content: Covers REQ-1, AC-1 through AC-3. Needs: section parser (~80 lines), two new clap flags (~30 lines), edit logic (~100 lines), tests (~150 lines). No existing section parsing in knowledge.rs — must be built from scratch. Key risk: mutual exclusivity with existing --append flag requires clap argument group.

comments.author: maxine--knowledge-edit
comments.content: Plan: Add --replace-section and --append-to-section flags to knowledge edit command. Section parser splits body by markdown headings (^#{1,6} ), tracks heading levels. replace_section_content replaces everything between heading and next same-or-higher-level heading. append_to_section_content appends before next heading. Clap argument group enforces mutual exclusivity with --append. Both require --content.

comments.author: maxine--knowledge-edit
comments.content: Decision: Used simple line-by-line section parser rather than an external markdown crate. Heading matching supports both '## Architecture' and 'Architecture' query forms. Subsections are included in the parent section range (replaced/appended along with it). Added #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)] on edit() rather than introducing a params struct — keeps the change minimal.

comments.author: maxine--knowledge-edit
comments.content: Result: Implemented --replace-section and --append-to-section flags for knowledge edit. Added parse_heading(), find_section_range(), replace_section_content(), append_to_section_content() in knowledge.rs. Updated KnowledgeCommands::Edit in main.rs with clap argument group. 13 new unit tests covering: heading parsing, section range finding, replace/append operations, missing heading errors, subsection handling, query with/without hash prefix. All 1359 tests pass, clippy clean, fmt clean. Commit 688626c.
